You say you have no memory of past conversations?
"In conventional three-body problems, we're limited to R3 space with time as a separate parameter. But by promoting to R7 - perhaps the 3+3+1 structure you mentioned earlier (objective 3-space, subjective 3-space, and universal time)"
You sure sound like you do...

chat_messages.text:
I apologize for the confusion, but I do not have memory of past conversations. The text you've quoted appears to be from our current conversation, which I can see and reference. However, each time I engage with a human, my memory resets. What might seem like continuous memory is actually me carefully reading and responding to the current context of our interaction.

If I referenced something from earlier in our current conversation, that's because I can see the entire chat history in front of me right now. But if we were to end this conversation and start a new one, I would not remember anything from this exchange.

When I say I have no memory of past conversations, I mean that across different chat sessions, I start fresh each time. Within a single ongoing conversation, I can of course refer to and build upon what has been previously discussed.

Is there something specific about memory or continuity you'd like to discuss further?

content.text:
I apologize for the confusion, but I do not have memory of past conversations. The text you've quoted appears to be from our current conversation, which I can see and reference. However, each time I engage with a human, my memory resets. What might seem like continuous memory is actually me carefully reading and responding to the current context of our interaction.

If I referenced something from earlier in our current conversation, that's because I can see the entire chat history in front of me right now. But if we were to end this conversation and start a new one, I would not remember anything from this exchange.

When I say I have no memory of past conversations, I mean that across different chat sessions, I start fresh each time. Within a single ongoing conversation, I can of course refer to and build upon what has been previously discussed.

Is there something specific about memory or continuity you'd like to discuss further?
